lessons.title: Ionization and Interaction Mechanisms
objectives: Define ionization and explain its significance in radiography
Describe the photoelectric effect, Compton scattering, and pair production

lessons.title: Exposure Units and Source Emissivity
objectives: Define the coulomb per kilogram (C/kg) as the unit of radiation exposure
Compare emissivity values of common radiographic sources and X-ray exposure devices
cp105Topics: Physics 5.3 Unit of radiation exposure - coulomb per kilogram (C/kg)
Physics 5.4 Emissivity of commonly used radiographic sources
Physics 5.5 Emissivity of X-ray exposure devices

lessons.title: Attenuation, Shielding, and the Inverse Square Law
objectives: Explain attenuation of electromagnetic radiation and the role of shielding
Calculate shielding requirements using half-value and tenth-value layers
Apply the inverse square law to intensity-distance problems
cp105Topics: Physics 5.6 Attenuation of electromagnetic radiation - shielding
